string.name: whats_new_content
string.translation_description: HTML content listing new features in this release, shown in the What's new dialog and appended to the Help screen. Please do not change the < and > markup.

resources.string:
Celestial Images

Info cards now include photographs for all labeled objects — planets, stars, constellations, and deep-sky objects. Tap any object to explore it visually.

New Catalog Objects

T Coronae Borealis (the "Blaze Star") and the Eta Carinae Nebula are now in the catalog.

Night Mode

Red night mode now covers nearly all UI elements, including all dialogs and the compass calibration screen — no more blinding white flashes in the dark.

string.name: beta_user_help_text
string.translation_description: HTML content shown to beta testers asking for feedback. Please do not change the < and > markup.

resources.string:
Beta users!

How you can help

In this release when you use manual mode and give the map a swipe, the horizon will return to level so the top of the map will always show the stars above you. Previously the map just moved in a straight line so you could end up upside down. What do you think? Is this better? Let us know here.
Open the help menu to see this information again.
